content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
June 2, 1989

Charles E. Knutsen
Democratic Party of the
San Fernando Valley
9140 Van Nuys Blvd., Suite 213
Panorama City, CA 91402

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-117

Dear Mr. Knutsen:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Democratic Party
of the San Fernando Valley (DPSFV) concerning the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTION

May organizations or political committees contribute to DPSFV
in excess of the contribution limitations established by
Proposition 73 for the purpose of publishing a slate mailer which
endorses Democratic candidates?

CONCLUSION

Organizations and political committees are limited to
contributing no more than $2,500 in a fiscal year to DPSFV to
support candidates for elective office. Because the slate mailers
are produced "at the behest" of the candidates endorsed, DPSFV's
payments to produce the slate mailers are contributions to those
candidates endorsed on the slate mailer.

FACTS

Your committee receives contributions from organizations and
other political committees. Although your committee does not
involve itself with campaigns, it does produce a slate mailer for
the purpose of electing Democratic candidates to office. The
slate mailer is "self-funded."

According to your telephone conversation with Carla Wardlow
of the Commission on May 11, 1989, no candidates pay to be on the
slate mailer and no committee or person makes payments to DPSFV at
the behest of any candidate. In addition, no payments are
received from any source for the purpose of having a specific
candidate included in the slate mailer.

¹Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Knutsen, Charles E.
Page Two

Candidates are interviewed and then DPSVF decides who to
endorse.

ANALYSIS

Section 82015 states that:

An expenditure made at the behest of a candidate, committee
or elected officer is a contribution to the candidate,
committee or elected officer unless full and adequate
consideration is received for making the expenditure.

Regulation 18215 defines "made at the behest" as:

A payment made under the control or at the direction of, in
cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, or
at the request or suggestion of a candidate, controlled
committee, official committee of a political party, or
organization formed or existing primarily for political
purposes.

Since there is coordination between DPSVF and candidates
concerning the production of the slate mailer, DPSVF's payments to
produce the slate mailers are considered made at the behest of
those candidates. (Rutberg Advice Letter, No. A-87-292, copy
enclosed.)

The Act limits the amount a committee may accept from a
person to $2,500 in a fiscal year (July 1- June 30) to make
contributions to candidates for elective office. (Section 85302.)
Therefore, payments received by DPSVF for the purpose of producing
slate mailers may not exceed $2,500 per contributor in a fiscal
year.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathleen E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

DEMOCRATIC PARTY
OF THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY
9440 Van Nuys Blvd., Suite 213 • Panorama City, California 91402 • (818) 893-9833

FEB 21 0 25 AM '89

February 16, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Office
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

ATTN: Kevin

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard:

Pursuant to our conversation this morning, I am request-
ing a written reply to my question regarding PAC contri-
butions to fund a slotemailer distributed by our organ-
ization.

My question has to do with the amount of monies PACs are
allowed to contribute. Is there a limit in our situation
according to Propositions 68 & 73 or may organizations
or PACs contribute, to us, monies in excess of the
stated limits?

Our slotemailer is self-funded and a non-profit item. We
do not, as an organization, become involved with campaigns.
The sole purpose of our slotemailer is elect Democrats.

Although, I received a verbal answer from you today, my
treasurer would like the information in writing.

I would appreciate an answeras soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Charles E. Knutsen, Chair

84-114

★ ★ ★ ★ ★ DEMOCRATIC PARTY
OF THE SAN FERNANDO VALLEY
9140 Van Nuys Blvd., Suite 213 ● Panorama City, California 91402 ● (818) 893-9833

FPC
FEB 21 825 AM '89

February 16, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Office
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

ATTN: Kevin

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard:

Pursuant to our conversation this morning, I am requesting a written reply to my question regarding PAC contributions to fund a slacemailer distributed by our organization.

My question has to do with the amount of monies PACs are allowed to contribute. Is there a limit in our situation according to Propositions 68 & 73 or may organizations or PACs contribute, to us, monies in excess of the stated limits?

Our slacemailer is self-funded and a non-profit item. We do not, as an organization, become involved with campaigns. The sole purpose of our slacemailer is elect Democrats.

Although, I received a verbal answer from you today, my treasurer would like the information in writing.

I would appreciate an answeras soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Charles E. Knutsen, Chair

February 22, 1989


Charles E. Knutsen, Chair
Democratic Party of the
   San Fernando Valley
9140 Van Nuys Blvd., Suite 213
Panorama City, CA 91402

                                          Re: Letter No. 89-117

Dear Mr. Knutsen:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on February 21, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                          Very truly yours,


                                          [signature]
                                          Jeanne Pritchard
                                          Chief Technical Assistance
                                          and Analysis Division
